notes: A single transcript event is unusually large; the parser ingests it without stalling and the session still settles ready→working→ready. aider is FilesUnderCWD{.aider.chat.history.md, RawLineParser} — one proc-<PID> session keyed by the process scanner, no UUID-keyed rollout; the whole arc (turn, pid_discovered, teardown) lands on that single proc-<PID> row. The oversized-line robustness is SHARED tailer code, not an aider behavior: TailAndProcess calls readLineCapped(reader, maxTranscriptLineSize=64MB) BEFORE the isRawLine branch routes the trimmed line into aider's ParseLineRaw, so the RawLineParser path is gated by exactly the same 64 MB cap (issue #270 hardening: bufio.NewReaderSize + readLineCapped, not bufio.Scanner) as the JSONL adapters — lines under 64 MB are buffered whole and resolved via the usual `> Tokens:`/IdleFlush turn close, lines over it are logged-and-skipped past without stalling. Recorded with the INTERACTIVE aider driver (tmux REPL): one send + wait_turn keeps the REPL alive so the daemon observes the working→ready settle (synthesized by IdleFlush after ~1500ms quiet, clamped to the ~5s SessionDetector working-refresh poll — hence the ≥8s trailing sleep), then a single tmux kill-session teardown yields one process_exited. ROBUSTNESS CLAIM: the 'oversized line' size property is NOT a phase-DSL form; the absence of a parser stall is INFERRED from this clean arc completing (no stuck working, no waiting). The genuine >64 MB cap behavior is pinned agent-agnostically by core/pkg/tailer/large_line_test.go (readLineCapped / oversized-skip / partial-at-EOF, issue #270) — a live aider CLI cannot be coerced into a multi-megabyte single line (markdown prose wraps; tool output is multi-line `>` blockquotes), so this recording is a sanity-check that the arc completes on a large single-line turn. DOCUMENTED TARGET: aider has no auth configured in the recording environment (no provider key; headless aider hangs on the model-auth prompt; the rig precheck delegates auth to the user), so the recording is deferred — this expected.jsonl is the authoritative state-arc target the cell will validate against once auth is available.

text: Session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch
Prompt eliciting one large single-line reply drives ready → working (binds the FIRST working = the proc-<PID> session)

Oversized assistant line is buffered and parsed without stalling (or skipped past, under the 64 MB cap) → turn completes via IdleFlush, working → ready; state never entered waiting. Completion of this transition is the spec's parser-resilience proof: if the tailer had wedged on the long line this would never fire.
PID bound to the proc-<PID> session at birth (aider binds PID when the process is discovered, not at turn start)
aider exits at REPL teardown — single process_exited on the proc-<PID> session
